title: Umami Crunchy Thai Mustard Greens Noodles
description: A bitter, crunchy, hangover‑cure Thai dish with fermented jang and danmuji, packed with anti‑inflammatory flavors and a delightful umami kick.
instructions: Cook the boat noodles according to package directions, drain and set aside.
Heat the sesame oil in a wok over medium heat and add the minced garlic, sauté until fragrant.
Add the fermented jang, fish sauce, tamarind paste, and brown sugar to the wok, stir to combine, then pour in water and bring to a gentle simmer.
Add the mustard greens, danmuji, and shredded carrots to the wok, cook until the greens wilt and the carrots soften.
Toss in the cooked noodles, stirring until everything is evenly coated and heated through.
Season the mixture with lime juice, chili flakes, and a pinch of salt if desired.
Remove the wok from heat, sprinkle toasted sesame seeds and chopped cilantro on top.
Plate the noodles on a talavera dish and serve immediately.
